goal: Suggest preprocessing and feature-engineering actions for predicting y from anonymized manufacturing test-bench features under the R2 metric using only official competition files.

task_characteristics.analysis_notes: Small single-table regression task with 4,209 training rows, 4,209 test rows, and a 378-column benchmark-relevant union across the official CSVs. The released table mixes eight low-cardinality categorical columns (X0, X1, X2, X3, X4, X5, X6, X8), a submission identifier ID, continuous target y, and a very wide sparse binary / integer feature block. The curated bank keeps the primary good spine conservative around ID removal, one categorical-encoding strategy, and deterministic zero-variance pruning. Wider selection, projection, and scaling branches remain rewarded only in model-specific stages. preprocessing_complexity_raw is authored as the retained good-row count before equivalence-group collapse.

dataset_description.summary: Official scope is limited to train.csv, test.csv, and sample_submission.csv already mirrored under this task root. The benchmark is official-only, single-table, and excludes public-kernel side inputs or any external manufacturing metadata.
dataset_description.raw_text: train.csv contains 4,209 labeled rows with identifier ID, regression target y, eight released categorical features (X0, X1, X2, X3, X4, X5, X6, X8), and hundreds of anonymized sparse binary / integer features through X385. test.csv contains the same predictors except for y. sample_submission.csv defines the ID,y submission format. The benchmark-relevant case-insensitive column union across these official files is 378. The official train table contains 12 deterministic constant columns: X11, X93, X107, X233, X235, X268, X289, X290, X293, X297, X330, and X347. Notebook prose often says X0-X8, but the released official tables do not contain X7.
